item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found this at random a while ago and have always loved this article due to troublesome past experiences with geese.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like me some untitled SCP game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See, I would have thought most instances of SCP-4135-A die when their house collapses on top of them or a SCP-4135-B smothers them in their sleep.

Or would those still count as "natural causes" for the purposes of this document?
My keter ducks finally made it! Quack quack

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To me, it's just the surreal humour of it.

There might be more to it, but just how bizarre it is makes me love it even more.

item.title: Re: Why is this not a -J?
item.content:encoded: -J articles aren't funny articles, they're joke articles. Articles you aren't supposed to see as fitting in the canon or take seriously. This article is funny but stays within mainlist logic and reason. I call them "Funny Mainlists". Prime examples would be 1370 and 1974.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, for personal reasons!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would make the argument that the article would just be muddied up if it took that route. Those who have been attacked by a goose before can probably relate to this article on an emotional level.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: exaqt, I've cleared the tags. If you're not going to add all the tags for an article, it's best to just leave the page untagged and wait for an experienced tagger to take care of it. We have a team of people on the site who tag articles, and they search for pages that have no tags. Adding only a few tags prevents the team from finding the article, so if you're unfamiliar with the tags, it's totally fine to wait for them to take care of it. (Also, "series-v" is not a tag that's actually used by the wiki.)

item.title: Hope you don't mind.
item.content:encoded: I found this article untagged, and added the tags keter and series-v just now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Anything could kill me. Hell, a dedicated duck could kill me!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I missing something here? Because this article is just geese manifesting over people and trying to kill them, I do not find that remotely interesting.

item.title: Why is this not a -J?
item.content:encoded:
There's nothing wrong with the article, I like it quite a bit in fact, but I think it should be a -J article. Not because it couldn't be canon (What ever that even means any more), but because it manages to be funny without sacrificing the SCP nature of it.

Upvoted regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

I have to wonder how the Foundation know this, especially given that the MTF has to search out and locate a new SCP-4135-A instance. Doesn't seem like the International Mallard Location Index is enough, as I currently understand it.

One of the biggest struggles i had with the IMLI is that i didn't find a way to explain what it did in a way that didn't screw up the pacing. I was thinking a footnote, but eh…

The IMLI is a catalog of civilian submitted reports that tracks reports of strange duck behsviors, and the foundation uses this to find reports that can suggest 4135-B instances attacking someone, then the foundation will react accordingly. They have only seen reports of one murder duck at a time, so they just assume.

I'll see if i can fit it in later tonight, to avoid confusion.

Until what breaks? Could be the container or the duck's head, the structure of the sentence isn't clear enough.

Yeah, that's poor wording on my part.

but "viscera" isn't typically reserved for brain matter and is more of a GI/abdominal term.

I always thought it was an umbrella term for "stuff that really should stay in your body." I dunno, i think i just like the term "duck viscera." I'll see if i can replace it with something more appropriate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the "Duck Hunt" MTF, the article is not poorly written, ducks can be real assholes, and the image of an invincible duck ceaselessly beating its head against a containment wall is enjoyably surreal.

Questions
– hide block

"and, almost instantaneously, another random human on Earth will become SCP-4135-A."

I have to wonder how the Foundation know this, especially given that the MTF has to search out and locate a new SCP-4135-A instance. Doesn't seem like the International Mallard Location Index is enough, as I currently understand it.

When an SCP-4135-B instance is trapped, such as if it is in a container, it will bash its head against the walls of the container until it breaks.

Until what breaks? Could be the container or the duck's head, the structure of the sentence isn't clear enough. I think it's the duck's head, taking a cue from the last sentence of the containment procedures, but "viscera" isn't typically reserved for brain matter and is more of a GI/abdominal term.

I don't have anything negative to say about this per se, and I enjoy simple & succinct articles, but I don't think the density of the anomaly justifies its abruptness. It's quirky and likable, but there's not a lot of substance. It's like an aphorism that has no punch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahhh I'm glad I clicked this - I was excited to see it go up when I saw it in the draft forums. Like I said earlier, I've been liking short articles like this with evocative word choice recently. Can't last forever, but it is entertaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't hate this, but it's essentially a more long-winded version of something the Far Side summed up in one line 30 years ago + violence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Crocket, anyway, it's still a good article that has my Upvote. Congratulations Westrin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know the joke was that it's an invulnerable duck that is just duck-strength, but it would've been way funnier if it was like a serial killed duck with ever-changing rediculous tactics with which it uses to kill people. Upvote, but a half-hearted one.

That pic is still glorious tho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So some poor bastard is continuously attacked by an invulnerable duck until he dies? I like it.
Geese can be troublesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Truly magnificent creatures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How anyone can like geese is beyond me.

Fantastic SCP Westrin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do not see the tags…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Get yourself a duck that can survive a nuclear attack

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
image stuff
– hide block

Image is CC BY SA 3.0

Source.


An SCP-4135-B instance chasing SCP-4135-A shortly after being shot by machine gun fire.


Do you hear that? Is that someone knocking on your door, or a duck violently bashing its head against your door?

Thanks to Uncle Nicolini, Crocket_Lawnchair, magnadeus, KindlyTurtleClem and many others for looking at this before it went up!

This most likely won't work. But if it does: quack quack.

Art

Oh god look out behind you
By Vorogib

More of their stuff can be found here.
